etymology.text: From Middle Iranian; see بنفشه (banafše) and compare Middle Persian wnpšk' (wanafšag, “violet”).

body.text: of Linn: and accord. to Forskål (Flora AEgypt. Arab. p. ciii.) applied in El-Yemen to the “
”] what is thus called is well known:
the smelling it in its fresh state is beneficial to those who are heated by wrath
and the continual smelling of it induces good sleep: the conserve made of it is beneficial for the pleurisy
and for inflammation of the lungs
and for cough, and for headache.

entries.lexicon_en: Al-Fayyūmī, Al-Miṣbāḥ al-Munīr fī Gharīb al-Sharḥ al-Kabīr (d. 1368 CE)
entries.body_html: ب ن ف س ج : الْبَنَفْسَجُ وِزَانُ سَفَرْجَلٍ مُعَرَّبٌ وَالْمُكَرَّرُ مِنْهُ اللَّامَاتُ وَوَزْنُهُ فَعْلَلَ.

entries.lexicon_en: Murtaḍa al-Zabīdī, Tāj al-ʿArūs fī Jawāhir al-Qamūs (d. 1790 CE)
entries.body_html:
بنفسج
: (البَنَفْسَجُ: م، شَمُّه رَطْباً يَنْفَعُ المَحْرُورِينَ، وإِدَامَةُ شَمِّه يُنَوِّمُ نَوْماً صَالِحاً، ومُرَبّاهُ يَنْفعُ مِنْ) وَجعِ (ذاتِ الجَنْبِ وذَاتِ الرِّثَةِ) وَهُوَ (نافعٌ للسُّعالِ والصُّداعِ) ، وتفصيلُه فِي كتب الطِّبّ.

entries.lexicon_en: Arabic-English Lexicon by Edward William Lane (d. 1876)
entries.lexicon_ar: المعجم العربي الإنجليزي لإدوارد وليام لين
entries.body_html:
بنفسج



بَنَفْسَجٌ, of the measure قَعَلَّلٌ, like سَفَرْجَلٌ, (Msb,) [an arabicized word, from the Persian بَنَفْشَ; The violet; viola odorata of Linn: and accord. to Forskål (Flora AEgypt. Arab. p. ciii.) applied in El-Yemen to the “ iris: ” and (p. cxx.) “ tagetes dubia? ”] what is thus called is well known: the smelling it in its fresh state is beneficial to those who are heated by wrath (المَحْرُورِين), and the continual smelling of it induces good sleep: the conserve made of it is beneficial for the pleurisy (ذَاتُ الجَنْبِ), and for inflammation of the lungs (ذَاتُ الرِّئَةِ), and for cough, and for headache. (K.)
